question_html:
Identify the parts of speech of the underlined words of the following sentence:

(a) People from all walks of life ended the meeting.

(b) Thou art not so unkind. 

(c) Adolescents are different both from young children and adults.

(d) He is small but mighty. 

(e) The very idea of it is absurd.
solution_html:
(a) noun

(b) verb

(c) adjective

(d) adjective

(e) adjective

Mark Distribution:

(0.5×5) = 2.5 marks for identifying the correct parts of speech of the underlined words.

question_html:
Identify the type of gender of the underlined words in the following sentences:

(a) The Chairman arrived on time at the meeting.

(b) They hired a companion for their grandmother.

(c) The horse cleared the hurdle.

(d) We rarely get to see any milkmaid today.

(e) His computer is out of order.
solution_html:
(a) masculine (b) common (c) masculine (d) feminine (e) neuter

Marks Distribution:

For identifying the correct type of gender of the underlined words (0.5×5) =2.5 marks.

[N.B. Any kind of mistake bears no marks. But mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.]

question_html:
Explain scientific inventions shortly.

Artificial Intelligence, Genetic Engineering
solution_html:
Artificial Intelligence (AI):
‎Artificial Intelligence refers to computer systems designed to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence, such as learning, reasoning, problem-solving, and language understanding. AI technologies power applications like virtual assistants, medical diagnosis, autonomous vehicles, and facial recognition. Recent advances in machine learning and neural networks have accelerated AI’s capabilities, making it integral to industries like healthcare, finance, and robotics. AI continues to evolve rapidly, promising to transform daily life, work, and scientific research worldwide.

Marks Distribution:

For writing the title ‘Artificial Intelligence (AI)’; 0.5 mark.

For writing a clear definition of Artificial Intelligence; 0.5 mark.

For mentioning real-life applications or uses of AI; 0.5 mark.

For stating how AI is transforming or will transform life and industries; 01 mark.

[N.B: 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more spelling mistakes.]

Genetic Engineering:
‎Genetic engineering is the deliberate modification of an organism’s DNA to alter its characteristics or introduce new traits. Techniques like CRISPR-Cas9 allow precise gene editing for medical treatments, agricultural improvements, and disease resistance. Genetic engineering has enabled the production of insulin, genetically modified crops, and potential cures for genetic disorders. While offering enormous benefits, it also raises ethical questions about safety, biodiversity, and human intervention in natural processes.

Marks Distribution:

For writing the title ‘Genetic Engineering’ 0.5 mark.

For writing a clear definition of what genetic engineering is; 0.5 mark.

For mentioning the practical uses or benefits of genetic engineering; 01 mark.

For writing a final statement on genetic engineering; 0.5 mark.

[N.B: 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more spelling mistakes.]
